# ferriskey_user (Resource)

Manages a user within a FerrisKey realm.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ferriskey_user" "alice" {
  realm          = "acme"
  username       = "alice"
  email          = "alice@acme.example"
  email_verified = true
  firstname      = "Alice"
  lastname       = "Liddell"
  enabled        = true

  required_actions = ["verify_email"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `realm` (String) Name of the realm the user belongs to. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `username` (String) Username. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `email` (String) Email address. Once set it cannot be cleared by removing it from the configuration (the API treats an omitted field as unchanged); set a new value to change it.
- `email_verified` (Boolean) Whether the email address is verified. Defaults to `false`.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the user is enabled. Defaults to `true`.
- `firstname` (String) First name.
- `lastname` (String) Last name.
- `required_actions` (Set of String) Set of required actions the user must complete at next login (`configure_otp`, `verify_email`, `update_password`, `configure_passkey`).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Composite identifier `{realm}/{uuid}`.
- `user_uuid` (String) Server-assigned UUID of the user.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import ferriskey_user.alice acme/9f1c0e2a-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-ddddeeeeffff
```
